I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Internal compiler error when using groupshared with interface-typed variables

## Issue Description

Declaring a `groupshared` variable with an interface type causes an internal compiler error (ICE 99999) during code emission on both SPIRV and HLSL targets. The compiler should either support this pattern or produce a clear diagnostic during semantic checking.

## Reproducer Code

```hlsl
interface IFoo
{
    float getValue();
}

struct ImplA : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v; }
}

struct ImplB : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v * 2.0; }
}

groupshared IFoo sharedVal;

RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;

[numthreads(64, 1, 1)]
void computeMain(uint3 tid : SV_DispatchThreadID)
{
    if (tid.x == 0)
        sharedVal = ImplA(42.0);
    GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync();
    outputBuffer[tid.x] = sharedVal.getValue();
}
```

**Command:**
```bash
slangc test.slang -target spirv -stage compute -entry computeMain -o /dev/null
```

## Expected Behavior

Either a clear diagnostic error during semantic checking (e.g., "groupshared variables cannot have interface types"), or correct compilation if the pattern is intended to be supported.

## Actual Behavior

SPIRV target:
```
(0): error 99999: Slang compilation aborted due to an exception of N5Slang13InternalErrorE:
unimplemented: Unhandled local inst in spirv-emit:
let  %1 : %2 = lookupWitness(%3, %IFoox5FgetValue)
```

HLSL target:
```
test.slang(24): internal error 99999: unimplemented feature in Slang compiler:
unexpected IR opcode during code emit
    outputBuffer[tid.x] = sharedVal.getValue();
                                            ^
```

Arrays of interface types (`groupshared IFoo arr[4]`) produce the same ICE.

Note: wrapping the interface in a struct (`groupshared struct { IFoo member; }`) compiles and generates correct dynamic dispatch code, suggesting the underlying mechanism is feasible.

## Additional Context

Test issue #10145 
Test PR #10143 



</pr_description>

2026-05-28 18:09:50,832 - DEBUG - swea-tools - Retrieved state from environment: {'working_dir': '/testbed'}

  1080	                }
  1081	            }
  1082	        }
  1083	
  1084	        return false;
  1085	    }
  1086	
  1087	    // Specializing lookup on witness tables is a general
  1088	    // transformation that helps with both generic and
  1089	    // existential-based code.
  1090	    //
  1091	    bool maybeSpecializeWitnessLookup(IRLookupWitnessMethod* lookupInst)
  1092	    {
  1093	        // Note: While we currently have named the instruction
  1094	        // `lookup_witness_method`, the `method` part is a misnomer
  1095	        // and the same instruction can look up *any* interface
  1096	        // requirement based on the witness table that provides
  1097	        // a conformance, and the "key" that indicates the interface
  1098	        // requirement.
  1099	
  1100	        // We can only specialize in the case where the lookup
  1101	        // is being done on a concrete witness table, and not
  1102	        // the result of a `specialize` instruction or other
  1103	        // operation that will yield such a table.
  1104	        //
  1105	        // Since we unify the frontend such that all LookupDeclRef node
  1106	        // on a interface requirement will always be lowered to lookup
  1107	        // witness, it creats an exception that IRThisTypeWitness, a non-concrete
  1108	        // witness table, also need to be specialized. Otherwise, there is
  1109	        // no logic in the later passes can handle it. However, we know for
  1110	        // sure that the IRThisTypeWitness is only used to wrap an interface
  1111	        // type, therefore, it must be only used to exact the interface requirement.
  1112	        auto witnessTable = as<IRWitnessTable>(lookupInst->getWitnessTable());
  1113	        IRInterfaceType* interfaceType = nullptr;
  1114	        if (!witnessTable)
  1115	        {
  1116	            if (auto witnessTableSet = as<IRWitnessTableSet>(lookupInst->getWitnessTable()))
  1117	            {
  1118	                auto requirementKey = lookupInst->getRequirementKey();
  1119	
  1120	                HashSet<IRInst*> satisfyingValSet;
  1121	                bool skipSpecialization = false;
  1122	                forEachInSet(
  1123	                    module,
  1124	                    witnessTableSet,
  1125	                    [&](IRInst* instElement)
  1126	                    {
  1127	                        if (auto table = as<IRWitnessTable>(instElement))
  1128	                        {
  1129	                            if (auto satisfyingVal = findWitnessTableEntry(table, requirementKey))
  1130	                            {
  1131	                                satisfyingValSet.add(satisfyingVal);
  1132	                                return;
  1133	                            }
  1134	                        }
  1135	
  1136	                        // If we reach here, we didn't find a satisfying value.
  1137	                        skipSpecialization = true;
  1138	                    });
  1139	
  1140	                if (!skipSpecialization)
  1141	                {
  1142	                    IRBuilder builder(module);
  1143	                    auto setOp = getSetOpFromType(lookupInst->getDataType());
  1144	                    auto newSet = builder.getSet(setOp, satisfyingValSet);
  1145	                    addUsersToWorkList(lookupInst);
  1146	                    if (as<IRTypeSet>(newSet))
  1147	                    {
  1148	                        lookupInst->replaceUsesWith(builder.getUntaggedUnionType(newSet));
  1149	                        lookupInst->removeAndDeallocate();
  1150	                    }
  1151	                    else if (as<IRWitnessTableSet>(newSet))
  1152	                    {
  1153	                        lookupInst->replaceUsesWith(newSet);
  1154	                        lookupInst->removeAndDeallocate();
  1155	                    }
  1156	                    else
  1157	                    {
  1158	                        // Should not see any other case.
  1159	                        SLANG_UNREACHABLE("unexpected set kind");
  1160	                    }
  1161	
  1162	                    return true;
  1163	                }
  1164	                else
  1165	                    return false;
  1166	            }
  1167	            else if (auto thisTypeWitness = as<IRThisTypeWitness>(lookupInst->getWitnessTable()))
  1168	            {
  1169	                if (auto witnessTableType =
  1170	                        as<IRWitnessTableTypeBase>(thisTypeWitness->getDataType()))

   293	
   294	static IRInst* specializeWitnessLookup(IRLookupWitnessMethod* lookupInst)
   295	{
   296	    // We can only specialize in the case where the lookup
   297	    // is being done on a concrete witness table, and not
   298	    // the result of a `specialize` instruction or other
   299	    // operation that will yield such a table.
   300	    //
   301	    auto witnessTable = as<IRWitnessTable>(lookupInst->getWitnessTable());
   302	    if (!witnessTable)
   303	        return lookupInst;
   304	
   305	    // Because we have a concrete witness table, we can
   306	    // use it to look up the IR value that satisfies
   307	    // the given interface requirement.
   308	    //
   309	    auto requirementKey = lookupInst->getRequirementKey();
   310	    auto satisfyingVal = findWitnessTableEntry(witnessTable, requirementKey);
   311	
   312	    lookupInst->replaceUsesWith(satisfyingVal);
   313	    lookupInst->removeAndDeallocate();
   314	
   315	    return satisfyingVal;
   316	}
   317	
   318	// Resolve any specialization and translations.
   319	IRInst* _resolveInstRec(TranslationContext* ctx, IRInst* inst)
   320	{
   321	    if (!inst)
   322	        return nullptr;
   323	
   324	    // Don't attempt to resolve insts that are potentially recursive.
   325	    if (as<IRInterfaceType>(inst) || as<IRWitnessTable>(inst))
   326	    {
   327	        return inst;
   328	    }
   329	
   330	    // IRParam is a function-scope value, not a type or specialization construct.
   331	    // It can appear as an operand of ExtractExistentialType when interface-typed
   332	    // entry-point params remain at function scope (e.g. CUDA compute).
   333	    if (as<IRParam>(inst))
   334	        return inst;
   335	
   336	    List<IRInst*> operands;
   337	    bool changed = false;
   338	
   339	    IRBuilder builder(ctx->getModule());
   340	    IRWeakUse* instRef = builder.getWeakUse(inst);
   341	
   342	    // Make sure all operands are resolved.
   343	    for (UInt i = 0; i < inst->getOperandCount(); ++i)
   344	    {
   345	        auto operand = inst->getOperand(i);
   346	        auto resolvedOperand = ctx->resolveInst(operand);
   347	        if (resolvedOperand != operand)
   348	            changed = true;
   349	    }
   350	
   351	    // Extract effective inst post-resolution. (the inst may have changed).
   352	    IRInst* instWithCanonicalOperands = instRef->getOperand(0);
   353	
   354	    if (isEvaluableOpCode(instWithCanonicalOperands->getOp()))
   355	    {
   356	        if (auto constFoldedResult = tryConstantFoldInst(
   357	                ctx->getModule(),
   358	                ctx->getTargetProgram(),
   359	                instWithCanonicalOperands))
   360	        {
   361	            instWithCanonicalOperands->replaceUsesWith(constFoldedResult);
   362	            return constFoldedResult;
   363	        }
   364	        else
   365	        {
   366	            SLANG_UNEXPECTED(
   367	                "Something went wrong.. a global inst with evaluable opcode should have been "
   368	                "constant folded");
   369	        }
   370	    }
   371	    else
   372	    {
   373	        switch (instWithCanonicalOperands->getOp())
   374	        {
   375	        case kIROp_SizeOf:
   376	        case kIROp_AlignOf:
   377	        case kIROp_GetArrayLength:
   378	            if (tryReplaceInstUsesWithSimplifiedValue(
   379	                    ctx->getTargetProgram(),
   380	                    ctx->getModule(),
   381	                    instWithCanonicalOperands))
   382	            {
   383	                return instRef->getOperand(0);
   384	            }
   385	            break;
   386	        default:
   387	            break;
   388	        }
   389	    }
   390	
   391	    // At this point, we've resolved anything that can be translated & not in the global scope (i.e.
   392	    // things like arithmetic operations)
   393	    //
   394	    // If the instruction is not at module scope, it cannot be resolved further.
   395	    // This can happen when a generic is specialized with an existential/interface type,
   396	    // producing function-local instructions that reach this resolution logic.
   397	    // Return the instruction as-is and let later passes handle the diagnostic.
   398	    //
   399	    if (!as<IRModuleInst>(instWithCanonicalOperands->getParent()))
   400	        return instWithCanonicalOperands;
   401	
   402	    // TODO: Group these.
   403	    if (as<IRTranslateBase>(instWithCanonicalOperands) ||
   404	        as<IRTranslatedTypeBase>(instWithCanonicalOperands) ||
   405	        as<IRForwardDiffFuncType>(instWithCanonicalOperands) ||
   406	        as<IRBwdCallableFuncType>(instWithCanonicalOperands) ||
   407	        as<IRApplyForBwdFuncType>(instWithCanonicalOperands) ||
   408	        as<IRBackwardDiffFuncType>(instWithCanonicalOperands))
   409	    {
   410	        auto translateInst = ctx->maybeTranslateInst(instWithCanonicalOperands);
   411	        instWithCanonicalOperands->replaceUsesWith(translateInst);
   412	        return translateInst;
   413	    }
   414	
   415	    // Assume at this point that we have a specializable inst with resolved operands.
   416	    auto entry = builder.fetchCompilerDictionaryEntry(
   417	        ctx->getModule()->getTranslationDict(),
   418	        instWithCanonicalOperands);
   419	
   420	    if (auto specializedInst = entry->getValue())
   421	    {
   422	        instWithCanonicalOperands->replaceUsesWith(specializedInst);
   423	        return specializedInst;
   424	    }
   425	
   426	    auto memoize = [&](IRInst* resultInst)
   427	    {
   428	        builder.setCompilerDictionaryEntryValue(entry, resultInst);
   429	        return resultInst;
   430	    };
   431	
   432	    switch (instWithCanonicalOperands->getOp())
   433	    {
   434	    case kIROp_Specialize:
   435	        {
   436	            if (!isSetSpecializedGeneric(instWithCanonicalOperands))
   437	            {
   438	                auto specInst = cast<IRSpecialize>(instWithCanonicalOperands);
   439	                auto specResult = specializeGeneric(ctx->getSpecializationContext(), specInst);
   440	
   441	                if (specResult && as<IRGlobalValueWithCode>(specResult))
   442	                {
   443	                    // If we ended up with something that has code,
   444	                    // specialization may have opened up some simplification opportunities.
   445	                    //
   446	
   447	                    applySparseConditionalConstantPropagation(
   448	                        specResult,
   449	                        ctx->getTargetProgram(),
   450	                        ctx->getSink(),
   451	                        ctx);
   452	
   453	                    if (!unrollLoopsInFunc(
   454	                            ctx->getTargetProgram(),
   455	                            ctx->getModule(),
   456	                            as<IRGlobalValueWithCode>(specResult),
   457	                            ctx->getSink()))
   458	                        return nullptr;
   459	                }
   460	
   461	                if (specResult && specResult != specInst)
   462	                    specInst->replaceUsesWith(specResult);
   463	
   464	                // No need to memoize since specializeGeneric will already have memoized this.
   465	                return specResult;
   466	            }
   467	            break;
   468	        }
   469	    case kIROp_LookupWitnessMethod:
   470	        return memoize(
   471	            specializeWitnessLookup(cast<IRLookupWitnessMethod>(instWithCanonicalOperands)));
   472	    }
   473	
   474	    return instWithCanonicalOperands;
   475	}
   476	
   477	IRInst* TranslationContext::resolveInst(IRInst* inst)
   478	{
   479	    IRBuilder builder(irModule);
   480	    while (auto resolvedInst = _resolveInstRec(this, inst))
   481	    {
   482	        if (resolvedInst == inst)
   483	        {
   484	            return resolvedInst;
   485	        }
   486	
   487	        inst = resolvedInst;
   488	    }
   489	    return nullptr;
   490	}
   491	
   492	}; // namespace Slang
   493

    16	namespace Slang
    17	{
    18	
    19	// Represents a work item for packing `inout` or `out` arguments after a concrete call.
    20	struct ArgumentPackWorkItem
    21	{
    22	    enum Kind
    23	    {
    24	        Pack,
    25	        UpCast,
    26	    } kind = Pack;
    27	
    28	    // A `AnyValue` typed destination.
    29	    IRInst* dstArg = nullptr;
    30	    // A concrete value to be packed.
    31	    IRInst* concreteArg = nullptr;
    32	};
    33	
    34	bool isAnyValueType(IRType* type)
    35	{
    36	    if (as<IRAnyValueType>(type) || as<IRUntaggedUnionType>(type))
    37	        return true;
    38	    return false;
    39	}
    40	
    41	// Unpack an `arg` of `IRAnyValue` into concrete type if necessary, to make it feedable into the
    42	// parameter. If `arg` represents a AnyValue typed variable passed in to a concrete `out`
    43	// parameter, this function indicates that it needs to be packed after the call by setting
    44	// `packAfterCall`.
    45	IRInst* maybeUnpackArg(
    46	    IRBuilder* builder,
    47	    IRType* paramType,
    48	    IRInst* arg,
    49	    ArgumentPackWorkItem& packAfterCall)
    50	{
    51	    packAfterCall.dstArg = nullptr;
    52	    packAfterCall.concreteArg = nullptr;
    53	
    54	    // If either paramType or argType is a pointer type
    55	    // (because of `inout` or `out` modifiers), we extract
    56	    // the underlying value type first.
    57	    IRType* paramValType = paramType;
    58	    IRType* argValType = arg->getDataType();
    59	    IRInst* argVal = arg;
    60	    if (auto ptrType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(paramType))
    61	    {
    62	        paramValType = ptrType->getValueType();
    63	    }
    64	    auto argType = arg->getDataType();
    65	    if (auto argPtrType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(argType))
    66	    {
    67	        argValType = argPtrType->getValueType();
    68	    }
    69	
    70	
    71	    // Unpack `arg` if the parameter expects concrete type but
    72	    // `arg` is an AnyValue.
    73	    if (!isAnyValueType(paramValType) && isAnyValueType(argValType))
    74	    {
    75	        // if parameter expects an `out` pointer, store the unpacked val into a
    76	        // variable and pass in a pointer to that variable.
    77	        if (as<IRPtrTypeBase>(paramType))
    78	        {
    79	            auto tempVar = builder->emitVar(paramValType);
    80	            if (as<IRBorrowInOutParamType>(paramType))
    81	                builder->emitStore(
    82	                    tempVar,
    83	                    builder->emitUnpackAnyValue(paramValType, builder->emitLoad(arg)));
    84	
    85	            // tempVar needs to be unpacked into original var after the call.
    86	            packAfterCall.kind = ArgumentPackWorkItem::Kind::Pack;
    87	            packAfterCall.dstArg = arg;
    88	            packAfterCall.concreteArg = tempVar;
    89	            return tempVar;
    90	        }
    91	        else
    92	        {
    93	            return builder->emitUnpackAnyValue(paramValType, argVal);
    94	        }
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    // Reinterpret 'arg' if it is being passed to a parameter with
    98	    // a different type collection. For now, we'll approximate this
    99	    // by checking if the types are different, but this should be
   100	    // encoded in the types.
   101	    //
   102	    if (as<IRTaggedUnionType>(paramValType) && as<IRTaggedUnionType>(argValType) &&
   103	        paramValType != argValType)
   104	    {
   105	        // if parameter expects an `out` pointer, store the unpacked val into a
   106	        // variable and pass in a pointer to that variable.
   107	        if (as<IROutParamType>(paramType))
   108	        {
   109	            auto tempVar = builder->emitVar(paramValType);
   110	
   111	            // tempVar needs to be unpacked into original var after the call.
   112	            packAfterCall.kind = ArgumentPackWorkItem::Kind::UpCast;
   113	            packAfterCall.dstArg = arg;
   114	            packAfterCall.concreteArg = tempVar;
   115	            return tempVar;
   116	        }
   117	        else
   118	        {
   119	            SLANG_UNEXPECTED("Unexpected upcast for non-out parameter");
   120	        }
   121	    }
   122	    return arg;
   123	}
   124	
   125	IRStringLit* _getWitnessTableWrapperFuncName(IRModule* module, IRFunc* func)
   126	{
   127	    IRBuilder builderStorage(module);
   128	    auto builder = &builderStorage;
   129	    builder->setInsertBefore(func);
   130	    if (auto linkageDecoration = func->findDecoration<IRLinkageDecoration>())
   131	    {
   132	        return builder->getStringValue(
   133	            (String(linkageDecoration->getMangledName()) + "_wtwrapper").getUnownedSlice());
   134	    }
   135	    if (auto namehintDecoration = func->findDecoration<IRNameHintDecoration>())
   136	    {
   137	        return builder->getStringValue(
   138	            (String(namehintDecoration->getName()) + "_wtwrapper").getUnownedSlice());
   139	    }
   140	    return nullptr;
   141	}
   142	
   143	// Create a wrapper function that makes a specific function's signature match it's type in the
   144	// interface requirement.
   145	//
   146	// e.g. the signature of the function from the caller's side might look like: ((ThisType, float) ->
   147	// ThisType) while the actual implementation function might be ((FooImpl, float) -> FooImpl).
   148	//
   149	// The witness table wrapper will marshal from the union-types (ThisType) to the concrete types
   150	// (FooImpl) expected by the implementation.
   151	//
   152	IRFunc* emitWitnessTableWrapper(
   153	    IRModule* module,
   154	    IRInst* funcInst,
   155	    IRFuncType* effectiveFuncType,
   156	    IRFuncType* interfaceRequirementVal)
   157	{
   158	    auto funcTypeInInterface = interfaceRequirementVal;
   159	    auto targetFuncType = effectiveFuncType;
   160	
   161	    IRBuilder builderStorage(module);
   162	    auto builder = &builderStorage;
   163	    builder->setInsertBefore(funcInst);
   164	
   165	    auto wrapperFunc = builder->createFunc();
   166	    wrapperFunc->setFullType(interfaceRequirementVal);
   167	    if (auto func = as<IRFunc>(funcInst))
   168	        if (auto name = _getWitnessTableWrapperFuncName(module, func))
   169	            builder->addNameHintDecoration(wrapperFunc, name);
   170	
   171	    builder->setInsertInto(wrapperFunc);
   172	    auto block = builder->emitBlock();
   173	    builder->setInsertInto(block);
   174	
   175	    ShortList<IRParam*> params;
   176	    for (UInt i = 0; i < funcTypeInInterface->getParamCount(); i++)
   177	    {
   178	        params.add(builder->emitParam(funcTypeInInterface->getParamType(i)));
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    List<IRInst*> args;
   182	    List<ArgumentPackWorkItem> argsToPack;
   183	
   184	    SLANG_ASSERT(params.getCount() == (Index)targetFuncType->getParamCount());
   185	    for (UInt i = 0; i < targetFuncType->getParamCount(); i++)
   186	    {
   187	        auto wrapperParam = params[i];
   188	        // Type of the parameter in the callee.
   189	        auto funcParamType = targetFuncType->getParamType(i);
   190	
   191	        // If the implementation expects a concrete type
   192	        // (either in the form of a pointer for `out`/`inout` parameters,
   193	        // or in the form a value for `in` parameters, while
   194	        // the interface exposes an AnyValue type,
   195	        // we need to unpack the AnyValue argument to the appropriate
   196	        // concerete type.
   197	        ArgumentPackWorkItem packWorkItem;
   198	        auto newArg = maybeUnpackArg(builder, funcParamType, wrapperParam, packWorkItem);
   199	        args.add(newArg);
   200	        if (packWorkItem.concreteArg)
   201	            argsToPack.add(packWorkItem);
   202	    }
   203	    auto call = builder->emitCallInst(targetFuncType->getResultType(), funcInst, args);
   204	
   205	    // Pack all `out` arguments.
   206	    for (auto item : argsToPack)
   207	    {
   208	        auto anyValType = cast<IRPtrTypeBase>(item.dstArg->getDataType())->getValueType();
   209	        auto concreteVal = builder->emitLoad(item.concreteArg);
   210	        auto packedVal = (item.kind == ArgumentPackWorkItem::Kind::Pack)
   211	                             ? builder->emitPackAnyValue(anyValType, concreteVal)
   212	                             : upcastSet(builder, concreteVal, anyValType);
   213	        builder->emitStore(item.dstArg, packedVal);
   214	    }
   215	
   216	    // Pack return value if necessary.
   217	    if (!isAnyValueType(call->getDataType()) &&
   218	        isAnyValueType(funcTypeInInterface->getResultType()))
   219	    {
   220	        auto pack = builder->emitPackAnyValue(funcTypeInInterface->getResultType(), call);
   221	        builder->emitReturn(pack);
   222	    }
   223	    else if (call->getDataType() != funcTypeInInterface->getResultType())
   224	    {
   225	        auto reinterpret = upcastSet(builder, call, funcTypeInInterface->getResultType());
   226	        builder->emitReturn(reinterpret);
   227	    }
   228	    else
   229	    {
   230	        if (call->getDataType()->getOp() == kIROp_VoidType)
   231	            builder->emitReturn();
   232	        else
   233	            builder->emitReturn(call);
   234	    }
   235	    return wrapperFunc;
   236	}
   237	
   238	UInt getUniqueID(IRBuilder* builder, IRInst* inst)
   239	{
   240	    // Fallback.
   241	    return builder->getUniqueID(inst);
   242	}
   243	
   244	// Generate a single function that dispatches to each function in the collection.
   245	// The resulting function will have one additional parameter to accept the tag
   246	// indicating which function to call.
   247	//
   248	IRFunc* createDispatchFunc(
   249	    IRFuncType* dispatchFuncType,
   250	    Dictionary<IRInst*, std::pair<IRInst*, IRFuncType*>>& mapping)
   251	{
   252	    // Create a dispatch function with switch-case for each function
   253	    IRBuilder builder(dispatchFuncType->getModule());
   254	
   255	    // Consume the first parameter of the expected function type
   256	    List<IRType*> innerParamTypes;
   257	    for (auto paramType : dispatchFuncType->getParamTypes())
   258	        innerParamTypes.add(paramType);
   259	    innerParamTypes.removeAt(0); // Remove the first parameter (ID)
   260	
   261	    auto resultType = dispatchFuncType->getResultType();
   262	    auto innerDispatchFuncType = builder.getFuncType(innerParamTypes, resultType);
   263	
   264	    auto func = builder.createFunc();
   265	    builder.setInsertInto(func);
   266	    func->setFullType(dispatchFuncType);
   267	
   268	    auto entryBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   269	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   270	
   271	    auto idParam = builder.emitParam(builder.getUIntType());
   272	
   273	    // Create parameters for the original function arguments
   274	    List<IRInst*> originalParams;
   275	    for (Index i = 0; i < innerParamTypes.getCount(); i++)
   276	    {
   277	        originalParams.add(builder.emitParam(innerParamTypes[i]));
   278	    }
   279	
   280	    // Create default block
   281	    auto defaultBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   282	    builder.setInsertInto(defaultBlock);
   283	    if (resultType->getOp() == kIROp_VoidType)
   284	    {
   285	        builder.emitReturn();
   286	    }
   287	    else
   288	    {
   289	        // Return a default-constructed value
   290	        auto defaultValue = builder.emitDefaultConstruct(resultType);
   291	        builder.emitReturn(defaultValue);
   292	    }
   293	
   294	    // Go back to entry block and create switch
   295	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   296	
   297	    // Create case blocks for each function
   298	    List<IRInst*> caseValues;
   299	    List<IRBlock*> caseBlocks;
   300	
   301	    for (auto kvPair : mapping)
   302	    {
   303	        auto funcInst = kvPair.second.first;
   304	        auto effectiveFuncType = kvPair.second.second;
   305	        auto funcTag = kvPair.first;
   306	
   307	        // The different functions in the mapping may have different signatures,
   308	        // so we need to emit a wrapper that marshals the parameters to the expected types for
   309	        // each function.
   310	        //
   311	        auto wrapperFunc = emitWitnessTableWrapper(
   312	            funcInst->getModule(),
   313	            funcInst,
   314	            effectiveFuncType,
   315	            innerDispatchFuncType);
   316	
   317	        // Create case block
   318	        auto caseBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   319	        builder.setInsertInto(caseBlock);
   320	
   321	        List<IRInst*> callArgs;
   322	        auto wrappedFuncType = as<IRFuncType>(wrapperFunc->getDataType());
   323	        for (Index ii = 0; ii < originalParams.getCount(); ii++)
   324	        {
   325	            callArgs.add(originalParams[ii]);
   326	        }
   327	
   328	        // Call the specific function
   329	        auto callResult =
   330	            builder.emitCallInst(wrappedFuncType->getResultType(), wrapperFunc, callArgs);
   331	
   332	        if (resultType->getOp() == kIROp_VoidType)
   333	        {
   334	            builder.emitReturn();
   335	        }
   336	        else
   337	        {
   338	            builder.emitReturn(callResult);
   339	        }
   340	
   341	        caseValues.add(funcTag);
   342	        caseBlocks.add(caseBlock);
   343	    }
   344	
   345	    // Create flattened case arguments array
   346	    List<IRInst*> flattenedCaseArgs;
   347	    for (Index i = 0; i < caseValues.getCount(); i++)
   348	    {
   349	        flattenedCaseArgs.add(caseValues[i]);
   350	        flattenedCaseArgs.add(caseBlocks[i]);
   351	    }
   352	
   353	    // Create an unreachable block for the break block.
   354	    auto unreachableBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   355	    builder.setInsertInto(unreachableBlock);
   356	    builder.emitUnreachable();
   357	
   358	    // Go back to entry and emit switch
   359	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   360	    builder.emitSwitch(
   361	        idParam,
   362	        unreachableBlock,
   363	        defaultBlock,
   364	        flattenedCaseArgs.getCount(),
   365	        flattenedCaseArgs.getBuffer());
   366	
   367	    return func;
   368	}
   369	
   370	// Create a function that maps input integers to output integers based on the provided mapping.
   371	IRFunc* createIntegerMappingFunc(IRModule* module, Dictionary<UInt, UInt>& mapping, UInt defaultVal)
   372	{
   373	    // Emit a switch statement with the inputs as case labels and outputs as return values.
   374	
   375	    IRBuilder builder(module);
   376	
   377	    auto funcType =
   378	        builder.getFuncType(List<IRType*>({builder.getUIntType()}), builder.getUIntType());
   379	    auto func = builder.createFunc();
   380	    builder.setInsertInto(func);
   381	    func->setFullType(funcType);
   382	
   383	    auto entryBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   384	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   385	
   386	    auto param = builder.emitParam(builder.getUIntType());
   387	
   388	    // Create default block that returns defaultVal
   389	    auto defaultBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   390	    builder.setInsertInto(defaultBlock);
   391	    builder.emitReturn(builder.getIntValue(builder.getUIntType(), defaultVal));
   392	
   393	    // Go back to entry block and create switch
   394	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   395	
   396	    // Create case blocks for each input table
   397	    List<IRInst*> caseValues;
   398	    List<IRBlock*> caseBlocks;
   399	
   400	    for (auto item : mapping)
   401	    {
   402	        // Create case block
   403	        auto caseBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   404	        builder.setInsertInto(caseBlock);
   405	        builder.emitReturn(builder.getIntValue(builder.getUIntType(), item.second));
   406	
   407	        caseValues.add(builder.getIntValue(builder.getUIntType(), item.first));
   408	        caseBlocks.add(caseBlock);
   409	    }
   410	
   411	    // Create flattened case arguments array
   412	    List<IRInst*> flattenedCaseArgs;
   413	    for (Index i = 0; i < caseValues.getCount(); i++)
   414	    {
   415	        flattenedCaseArgs.add(caseValues[i]);
   416	        flattenedCaseArgs.add(caseBlocks[i]);
   417	    }
   418	
   419	    // Emit an unreachable block for the break block.
   420	    auto unreachableBlock = builder.emitBlock();
   421	    builder.setInsertInto(unreachableBlock);
   422	    builder.emitUnreachable();
   423	
   424	    // Go back to entry and emit switch
   425	    builder.setInsertInto(entryBlock);
   426	    builder.emitSwitch(
   427	        param,
   428	        unreachableBlock,
   429	        defaultBlock,
   430	        flattenedCaseArgs.getCount(),
   431	        flattenedCaseArgs.getBuffer());
   432	
   433	    return func;
   434	}
   435	
   436	// This context lowers `GetTagOfElementInSet`,
   437	// `GetTagForSuperSet`, and `GetTagForMappedSet` instructions,
   438	//
   439	struct TagOpsLoweringContext : public InstPassBase
   440	{
   441	    // Our strategy for lowering tag operations is to
   442	    // assign each element to a unique integer ID that is stable
   443	    // across the same module. This is acheived via `getUniqueID`,
   444	    // on the IRBuilder, which uses a dicionary on the module inst
   445	    // to keep track of assignments.
   446	    //
   447	    // Then, tag operations can be lowered to mapping functions that
   448	    // take an integer in and return an integer out, based on the
   449	    // input and output sets (and any other operands)
   450	    //
   451	    TagOpsLoweringContext(IRModule* module)
   452	        : InstPassBase(module)
   453	    {
   454	    }
   455	
   456	    void lowerGetTagForSuperSet(IRGetTagForSuperSet* inst)
   457	    {
   458	        // `GetTagForSuperSet` is a no-op since we want to translate the tag
   459	        // for an element in the sub-set to a tag for the same element in the super-set.
   460	        //
   461	        // Since all elements have a unique ID across the module, this is the identity operation.
   462	        //
   463	
   464	        IRBuilder builder(inst->getModule());
   465	        builder.setInsertAfter(inst);
   466	        inst->replaceUsesWith(builder.emitCast(inst->getDataType(), inst->getOperand(0), true));
   467	        inst->removeAndDeallocate();
   468	    }
   469	
   470	    void lowerGetTagForSubSet(IRGetTagForSubSet* inst)
   471	    {
   472	        // `GetTagForSubSet` is a no-op since we want to translate the tag
   473	        // for an element <response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

   539	
   540	    void lowerGetTagOfElementInSet(IRGetTagOfElementInSet* inst)
   541	    {
   542	        // `GetTagOfElementInSet` simply gets replaced by the element's
   543	        //  unique ID (as an integer literal value)
   544	        //
   545	        // Note: the element must be a concrete global inst (cannot by a
   546	        // dynamic value)
   547	        //
   548	        IRBuilder builder(inst->getModule());
   549	        builder.setInsertAfter(inst);
   550	
   551	        auto uniqueId = getUniqueID(&builder, inst->getOperand(0));
   552	        auto resultValue = builder.getIntValue(inst->getDataType(), uniqueId);
   553	        inst->replaceUsesWith(resultValue);
   554	        inst->removeAndDeallocate();
   555	    }
   556	
   557	    void processInst(IRInst* inst)
   558	    {
   559	        switch (inst->getOp())
   560	        {
   561	        case kIROp_GetTagForSuperSet:
   562	            lowerGetTagForSuperSet(as<IRGetTagForSuperSet>(inst));
   563	            break;
   564	        case kIROp_GetTagForSubSet:
   565	            lowerGetTagForSubSet(as<IRGetTagForSubSet>(inst));
   566	            break;
   567	        case kIROp_GetTagForMappedSet:
   568	            lowerGetTagForMappedSet(as<IRGetTagForMappedSet>(inst));
   569	            break;
   570	        case kIROp_GetTagOfElementInSet:
   571	            lowerGetTagOfElementInSet(as<IRGetTagOfElementInSet>(inst));
   572	            break;
   573	        default:
   574	            break;
   575	        }
   576	    }
   577	
   578	    void processModule()
   579	    {
   580	        processAllInsts([&](IRInst* inst) { return processInst(inst); });
   581	    }
   582	};
   583	
   584	// This context lowers `TypeSet` instructions.
   585	struct UntaggedUnionLoweringContext : public InstPassBase
   586	{
   587	    UntaggedUnionLoweringContext(
   588	        IRModule* module,
   589	        TargetProgram* targetProgram,
   590	        DiagnosticSink* sink = nullptr)
   591	        : InstPassBase(module), targetProgram(targetProgram), sink(sink)
   592	    {
   593	    }
   594	
   595	    SlangInt tryCalculateAnyValueSize(const HashSet<IRType*>& types)
   596	    {
   597	        SlangInt maxSize = 0;
   598	        for (auto type : types)
   599	        {
   600	            auto size = getAnyValueSize(type, targetProgram->getTargetReq());
   601	            if (size > maxSize)
   602	                maxSize = size;
   603	
   604	            // We need to consider whether the concrete type can be stored in an `AnyValue`.
   605	            //
   606	            // For example, resource types that are not bindless on the target cannot be marshalled
   607	            // and we need to diagnose that.
   608	            //
   609	            if (sink && !canTypeBeStored(type))
   610	            {
   611	                sink->diagnose(Diagnostics::TypeCannotBePackedIntoAnyValue{
   612	                    .type = type,
   613	                    .location = type->sourceLoc,
   614	                });
   615	            }
   616	        }
   617	
   618	        // Defaults to 0 if any type could not be sized.
   619	        return maxSize;
   620	    }
   621	
   622	    IRAnyValueType* createAnyValueType(IRBuilder* builder, const HashSet<IRType*>& types)
   623	    {
   624	        auto size = tryCalculateAnyValueSize(types);
   625	        return builder->getAnyValueType(size);
   626	    }
   627	
   628	    // Check whether a type (or any of its nested fields) contains types that
   629	    // cannot be marshalled to/from an AnyValue. Rejects types that would crash
   630	    // in emitMarshallingCode or produce invalid output for the current target.
   631	    bool containsUnmarshalableType(IRType* type)
   632	    {
   633	        if (getResolvedInstForDecorations(type)->findDecoration<IRNonCopyableTypeDecoration>())
   634	            return true;
   635	
   636	        switch (type->getOp())
   637	        {
   638	        case kIROp_AtomicType:
   639	        case kIROp_UnsizedArrayType:
   640	            return true;
   641	
   642	        case kIROp_PtrType:
   643	            {
   644	                // SPIRV generates incompatible struct types for Function vs
   645	                // PhysicalStorageBuffer storage classes. Pointers to concrete
   646	                // structs packed into AnyValue produce type mismatches in the
   647	                // generated SPIRV. CPU and CUDA handle this correctly.
   648	                // Interface pointers are allowed: they point to existential
   649	                // tuples that are already handled by dynamic dispatch lowering.
   650	                if (!isSPIRV(targetProgram->getTargetReq()->getTarget()))
   651	                    return false;
   652	                auto ptrType = cast<IRPtrTypeBase>(type);
   653	                auto valueType = ptrType->getValueType();
   654	                return valueType->getOp() != kIROp_InterfaceType;
   655	            }
   656	
   657	        case kIROp_StructType:
   658	            {
   659	                auto structType = cast<IRStructType>(type);
   660	                for (auto field : structType->getFields())
   661	                {
   662	                    if (containsUnmarshalableType(field->getFieldType()))
   663	                        return true;
   664	                }
   665	                return false;
   666	            }
   667	
   668	        case kIROp_ArrayType:
   669	            {
   670	                auto arrayType = cast<IRArrayType>(type);
   671	                return containsUnmarshalableType((IRType*)arrayType->getElementType());
   672	            }
   673	
   674	        case kIROp_VectorType:
   675	            {
   676	                auto vectorType = cast<IRVectorType>(type);
   677	                return containsUnmarshalableType((IRType*)vectorType->getElementType());
   678	            }
   679	
   680	        case kIROp_MatrixType:
   681	            {
   682	                auto matrixType = cast<IRMatrixType>(type);
   683	                return containsUnmarshalableType((IRType*)matrixType->getElementType());
   684	            }
   685	
   686	        default:
   687	            return false;
   688	        }
   689	    }
   690	
   691	    bool canTypeBeStored(IRType* concreteType)
   692	    {
   693	        if (!areResourceTypesBindlessOnTarget(targetProgram->getTargetReq()))
   694	        {
   695	            IRType* opaqueType = nullptr;
   696	            if (isOpaqueType(concreteType, &opaqueType))
   697	            {
   698	                return false;
   699	            }
   700	        }

     1	// slang-ir-typeflow-specialize.h
     2	#pragma once
     3	#include "slang-ir.h"
     4	#include "slang-target-program.h"
     5	
     6	namespace Slang
     7	{
     8	struct SpecializationContext;
     9	
    10	// Convert dynamic insts such as `LookupWitnessMethod`, `ExtractExistentialValue`,
    11	// `ExtractExistentialType`, `ExtractExistentialWitnessTable` and more into specialized versions
    12	// based on the possible values at at the use sites, based on a data-flow-style interprocedural
    13	// analysis.
    14	//
    15	// This pass is intended to be run after all specialization insts with concrete arguments have
    16	// already been processed.
    17	//
    18	// This pass may generate more `Specialize` insts, so it should be run in a loop with
    19	// the standard specialization pass until a no more changes can be made.
    20	//
    21	bool specializeDynamicInsts(
    22	    IRModule* module,
    23	    TargetProgram* target,
    24	    DiagnosticSink* sink,
    25	    SpecializationContext* context,
    26	    bool shouldReportDynamicDispatchSites);

  1750	
  1751	            if (sink && sink->getErrorCount() != 0)
  1752	                break;
  1753	
  1754	            if (iterChanged)
  1755	            {
  1756	                this->changed = true;
  1757	                eliminateDeadCode(module->getModuleInst());
  1758	                peepholeOptimizeGlobalScope(targetProgram, this->module);
  1759	                performMandatoryEarlyInlining(module);
  1760	                applySparseConditionalConstantPropagation(this->module, targetProgram, this->sink);
  1761	                unrollLoopsInModule(module, targetProgram, sink);
  1762	            }
  1763	
  1764	            if (sink && sink->getErrorCount() != 0)
  1765	                break;
  1766	
  1767	            // Once the work list has gone dry, we should have the invariant
  1768	            // that there are no `specialize` instructions inside of non-generic
  1769	            // functions that in turn reference a generic type/function unless the generic is
  1770	            // for a builtin type/function, or some of the type arguments are unknown at compile
  1771	            // time, in which case we will rely on a follow up pass the translate it into a
  1772	            // dynamic dispatch function.
  1773	            //
  1774	            // Now we consider lower lookupWitnessMethod insts into dynamic dispatch calls,
  1775	            // which may open up more specialization opportunities.
  1776	            //
  1777	            if (options.lowerWitnessLookups)
  1778	            {
  1779	                bool dynPassChanged = specializeDynamicInsts(
  1780	                    module,
  1781	                    targetProgram,
  1782	                    sink,
  1783	                    this,
  1784	                    options.reportDynamicDispatchSites);
  1785	
  1786	                if (dynPassChanged)
  1787	                    eliminateDeadCode(module->getModuleInst());
  1788	
  1789	                iterChanged |= dynPassChanged;
  1790	            }
  1791	
  1792	            if (!iterChanged || sink->getErrorCount())
  1793	                break;
  1794	        }
  1795	    }
  1796	
  1797	    void addInstsToWorkListRec(IRInst* inst)
  1798	    {
  1799	        addToWorkList(inst);
  1800	
  1801	        for (auto child = inst->getLastChild(); child; child = child->getPrevInst())
  1802	        {
  1803	            addInstsToWorkListRec(child);
  1804	        }
  1805	    }

So `emitGlobalInst` handles global (module-level) instructions and only handles types, constants, and other global values. It does NOT handle `IRLookupWitnessMethod`. But wait, the error message says "Unhandled local inst" - that's from `emitLocalInst`. So the issue is that `IRLookupWitnessMethod` appears inside a function body when emitting.

But the `legalize-global-values.cpp` pass says `IRLookupWitnessMethod` should NOT be inlined. So how does it end up inside a function body?

Let me look at the actual error more carefully. The error says:
```
let  %1 : %2 = lookupWitness(%3, %IFoox5FgetValue)
```

This looks like it could be generated inside a function due to the `sharedVal.getValue()` call. Let me think about this...

Actually, let me look at how the interface dispatch works. When you have a `groupshared IFoo sharedVal;` and call `sharedVal.getValue()`, the compiler needs to:
1. Load the witness table from the sharedVal existential
2. Look up the method in the witness table
3. Call the method

The `lookupWitness` instruction is produced when looking up a method from a witness table. If the witness table comes from a `groupshared` variable, the lookup is dynamic and cannot be resolved at compile time.

  5408	    }
  5409	
  5410	    bool specializeLookupWitnessMethod(IRInst* context, IRLookupWitnessMethod* inst)
  5411	    {
  5412	        // Handle trivial case where inst's operand is a concrete table.
  5413	        if (auto witnessTable = as<IRWitnessTable>(inst->getWitnessTable()))
  5414	        {
  5415	            inst->replaceUsesWith(findWitnessTableEntry(witnessTable, inst->getRequirementKey()));
  5416	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5417	            return true;
  5418	        }
  5419	
  5420	        // Otherwise, we go off the info for the inst.
  5421	        auto info = tryGetInfo(context, inst);
  5422	        if (!info)
  5423	            return false;
  5424	
  5425	        // If we didn't resolve anything for this inst, don't modify it.
  5426	        auto elementOfSetType = as<IRElementOfSetType>(info);
  5427	        if (!elementOfSetType)
  5428	            return false;
  5429	
  5430	        IRBuilder builder(inst);
  5431	        builder.setInsertBefore(inst);
  5432	
  5433	        // If there's a single element, we can do a simple replacement.
  5434	        if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isSingleton())
  5435	        {
  5436	            auto element = elementOfSetType->getSet()->getElement(0);
  5437	            inst->replaceUsesWith(element);
  5438	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5439	            return true;
  5440	        }
  5441	        else if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isEmpty())
  5442	        {
  5443	            auto poison = builder.emitPoison(inst->getDataType());
  5444	            inst->replaceUsesWith(poison);
  5445	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5446	            return true;
  5447	        }
  5448	        else if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isUnbounded())
  5449	        {
  5450	            // Leave the lookup as-is for unbounded sets, since this
  5451	            // will become an actual dynamic lookup at run-time.
  5452	            //
  5453	            return false;
  5454	        }
  5455	
  5456	        // If we reach here, we have a truly dynamic case. Multiple elements.
  5457	        // We need to emit a run-time inst to keep track of the tag.
  5458	        //
  5459	        // We use the GetTagForMappedSet inst to do this, and set its data type to
  5460	        // the appropriate tag-type.

  5492	        // If we have a non-trivial info registered, it must of
  5493	        // SetTagType(WitnessTableSet(...))
  5494	        //
  5495	        // Further, the operand must be an existential (TaggedUnionType), which is
  5496	        // conceptually a pair of TagType(tableSet) and a
  5497	        // UntaggedUnionType(typeSet)

  5499	        // We will simply extract the first element of this tuple.
  5500	        //
  5501	
  5502	        auto info = tryGetInfo(context, inst);
  5503	        if (!info)
  5504	            return false;
  5505

  5507	        builder.setInsertBefore(inst);
  5508	
  5509	        if (auto elementOfSetType = as<IRElementOfSetType>(info))
  5510	        {
  5511	            if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isSingleton())
  5512	            {
  5513	                // Found a single possible type. Simple replacement.
  5514	                inst->replaceUsesWith(elementOfSetType->getSet()->getElement(0));
  5515	                inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5516	                return true;
  5517	            }
  5518	            else if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isUnbounded())
  5519	            {
  5520	                // We'll leave extracts from unbounded sets alone.
  5521	                // This typically implies COM interfaces or other run-time dynamic
  5522	                // lookups. These are further handled in lowerComInterfaces()
  5523	                //

  5525	            }

2026-05-28 18:13:18,810 - INFO - swea-agent - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I understand the flow better. The `specializeLookupWitnessMethod` function can handle:

1. Concrete witness table (direct replacement)
2. Singleton sets (direct replacement)
3. Unbounded sets (leave as-is for further lowering)
4. Multi-element sets (transform to `GetTagForMappedSet`)

But if none of these conditions match, it returns false and the `IRLookupWitnessMethod` stays as-is.

Let me now create a test to reproduce the issue

🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/test_groupshared.slang << 'EOF'
interface IFoo
{
    float getValue();
}

struct ImplA : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v; }
}

struct ImplB : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v * 2.0; }
}

groupshared IFoo sharedVal;

RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;

[numthreads(64, 1, 1)]
void computeMain(uint3 tid : SV_DispatchThreadID)
{
    if (tid.x == 0)
        sharedVal = ImplA(42.0);
    GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync();
    outputBuffer[tid.x] = sharedVal.getValue();
}
EOF

  1688	                // We will then iterate until our work list goes dry.
  1689	                //
  1690	                while (workList.getCount() != 0)
  1691	                {
  1692	                    // If we've emitted errors (e.g. from diagnosing an invalid
  1693	                    // existential specialization), bail out early to avoid
  1694	                    // processing IR that may be in an inconsistent state.
  1695	                    if (sink && sink->getErrorCount() != 0)
  1696	                    {
  1697	                        workList.clear();
  1698	                        break;
  1699	                    }
  1700	
  1701	                    IRInst* inst = workList.getLast();
  1702	
  1703	                    workList.removeLast();
  1704	                    workListSet.remove(inst);
  1705	
  1706	                    if (!inst->getParent() && inst->getOp() != kIROp_ModuleInst)
  1707	                        continue;
  1708	
  1709	                    // For each instruction we process, we want to perform
  1710	                    // a few steps.
  1711	                    //
  1712	                    // First we will look for all the general-purpose
  1713	                    // specialization opportunities (generic specialization,
  1714	                    // existential specialization, simplifications, etc.)
  1715	                    //
  1716	                    if (inst->hasUses() || inst->mightHaveSideEffects() || isWitnessTableType(inst))
  1717	                    {
  1718	                        hasSpecialization |= maybeSpecializeInst(inst);
  1719	                    }
  1720	
  1721	                    // Finally, we need to make our logic recurse through
  1722	                    // the whole IR module, so we want to add the children
  1723	                    // of any parent instructions to our work list so that
  1724	                    // we process them too.
  1725	                    //
  1726	                    // Note that we are adding the children of an instruction
  1727	                    // in reverse order. This is because the way we are
  1728	                    // using the work list treats it like a stack (LIFO) and
  1729	                    // we know that fully-specialized-ness will tend to flow
  1730	                    // top-down through the program, so that we want to process
  1731	                    // the children of an instruction in their original order.
  1732	                    //
  1733	                    for (auto child = inst->getLastDecorationOrChild(); child;
  1734	                         child = child->getPrevInst())
  1735	                    {
  1736	                        // Also note that `addToWorkList` has been written
  1737	                        // to avoid adding any instruction that is a descendent
  1738	                        // of an IR generic, because we don't actually want
  1739	                        // to perform specialization inside of generics.
  1740	                        //
  1741	                        if (!isAnnotation(child))
  1742	                            addToWorkList(child);
  1743	                    }
  1744	                }
  1745	                if (hasSpecialization)
  1746	                    iterChanged = true;
  1747	                else
  1748	                    break;
  1749	            }
  1750	
  1751	            if (sink && sink->getErrorCount() != 0)
  1752	                break;
  1753	
  1754	            if (iterChanged)
  1755	            {
  1756	                this->changed = true;
  1757	                eliminateDeadCode(module->getModuleInst());
  1758	                peepholeOptimizeGlobalScope(targetProgram, this->module);
  1759	                performMandatoryEarlyInlining(module);
  1760	                applySparseConditionalConstantPropagation(this->module, targetProgram, this->sink);
  1761	                unrollLoopsInModule(module, targetProgram, sink);
  1762	            }
  1763	
  1764	            if (sink && sink->getErrorCount() != 0)
  1765	                break;
  1766	
  1767	            // Once the work list has gone dry, we should have the invariant
  1768	            // that there are no `specialize` instructions inside of non-generic
  1769	            // functions that in turn reference a generic type/function unless the generic is
  1770	            // for a builtin type/function, or some of the type arguments are unknown at compile
  1771	            // time, in which case we will rely on a follow up pass the translate it into a
  1772	            // dynamic dispatch function.
  1773	            //
  1774	            // Now we consider lower lookupWitnessMethod insts into dynamic dispatch calls,
  1775	            // which may open up more specialization opportunities.
  1776	            //
  1777	            if (options.lowerWitnessLookups)
  1778	            {
  1779	                bool dynPassChanged = specializeDynamicInsts(
  1780	                    module,
  1781	                    targetProgram,
  1782	                    sink,
  1783	                    this,
  1784	                    options.reportDynamicDispatchSites);
  1785

  3168	    IRInst* analyzeLookupWitnessMethod(IRInst* context, IRLookupWitnessMethod* inst)
  3169	    {
  3170	        // A LookupWitnessMethod is assumed to by dynamic, so we
  3171	        // (i) construct a set of the results by looking up the given
  3172	        //     key in each of the input witness tables
  3173	        // (ii) wrap the result in a tag type, since the lookup inst is logically holding
  3174	        //     on to run-time information about which element of the set is active.
  3175	        //
  3176	        // Note that the input must be a set of concrete witness tables (or none/unbounded).
  3177	        // If this is not the case and we see anything abstract, then something has gone
  3178	        // wrong somewhere when analyzing a previous instruction.
  3179	        //
  3180	
  3181	        auto key = inst->getRequirementKey();
  3182	
  3183	        auto witnessTable = inst->getWitnessTable();
  3184	        auto witnessTableInfo = tryGetInfo(context, witnessTable);
  3185	
  3186	        if (auto elementOfSetType = as<IRElementOfSetType>(witnessTableInfo))
  3187	        {
  3188	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  3189	            HashSet<IRInst*>& results = *module->getContainerPool().getHashSet<IRInst>();
  3190	            forEachInSet(
  3191	                module,
  3192	                cast<IRWitnessTableSet>(elementOfSetType->getSet()),
  3193	                [&](IRInst* table)
  3194	                {
  3195	                    if (as<IRUnboundedWitnessTableElement>(table))
  3196	                    {
  3197	                        if (inst->getDataType()->getOp() == kIROp_FuncType)
  3198	                            results.add(builder.getUnboundedFuncElement());
  3199	                        else if (inst->getDataType()->getOp() == kIROp_WitnessTableType)
  3200	                            results.add(builder.getUnboundedWitnessTableElement(
  3201	                                as<IRWitnessTableType>(inst->getDataType())->getConformanceType()));
  3202	                        else if (inst->getDataType()->getOp() == kIROp_TypeKind)
  3203	                        {
  3204	                            SLANG_UNEXPECTED(
  3205	                                "TypeKind result from LookupWitnessMethod not supported");
  3206	                        }
  3207	                        return;
  3208	                    }
  3209	
  3210	                    auto resolvedTable = translationContext.resolveInst(table);
  3211	
  3212	                    // TODO: Make 'none witness' values a proper thing instead of three different
  3213	                    // possibilities..
  3214	                    //
  3215	                    if (as<IRNoneWitnessTableElement>(table) || as<IRVoidLit>(table) ||
  3216	                        (as<IRWitnessTable>(resolvedTable)->getConformanceType()->getOp() ==
  3217	                         kIROp_VoidType))
  3218	                    {
  3219	                        results.add(builder.getVoidValue());
  3220	                        return;
  3221	                    }
  3222	
  3223	                    results.add(findWitnessTableEntry(cast<IRWitnessTable>(resolvedTable), key));
  3224	                });
  3225	
  3226	            auto setOp = getSetOpFromType(inst->getDataType());
  3227	            auto resultSetType = makeElementOfSetType(builder.getSet(setOp, results));
  3228	            module->getContainerPool().free(&results);
  3229	
  3230	            return resultSetType;
  3231	        }
  3232	
  3233	        if (!witnessTableInfo)
  3234	            return none();
  3235	
  3236	        SLANG_UNEXPECTED("Unexpected witness table info type in analyzeLookupWitnessMethod");
  3237	    }
  3238	
  3239	    // Check if an existential operand is an entry-point parameter of interface type
  3240	    // that lacks typeflow info. This catches targets (like CUDA compute) that skip

  5410	    bool specializeLookupWitnessMethod(IRInst* context, IRLookupWitnessMethod* inst)
  5411	    {
  5412	        // Handle trivial case where inst's operand is a concrete table.
  5413	        if (auto witnessTable = as<IRWitnessTable>(inst->getWitnessTable()))
  5414	        {
  5415	            inst->replaceUsesWith(findWitnessTableEntry(witnessTable, inst->getRequirementKey()));
  5416	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5417	            return true;
  5418	        }
  5419	
  5420	        // Otherwise, we go off the info for the inst.
  5421	        auto info = tryGetInfo(context, inst);
  5422	        if (!info)
  5423	            return false;
  5424	
  5425	        // If we didn't resolve anything for this inst, don't modify it.

  5427	        if (!elementOfSetType)

  5429

  5436	            auto element = elementOfSetType->getSet()->getElement(0);
  5437	            inst->replaceUsesWith(element);
  5438	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5439	            return true;
  5440	        }

So the issue is: when we have a `groupshared` variable with an interface type, the witness table lookup can't be resolved because the type information doesn't flow through the groupshared variable. The `specializeLookupWitnessMethod` returns false, leaving the `IRLookupWitnessMethod` in the IR. Then the SPIRV/HLS emit fails because they don't know how to handle this instruction.

The PR description says: "Either a clear diagnostic error during semantic checking (e.g., 'groupshared variables cannot have interface types'), or correct compilation if the pattern is intended to be supported."

The simplest approach is to add a diagnostic during semantic checking that prevents using interface types with `groupshared`. Let me look at how `groupshared` is checked semantically.

Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The PR description says wrapping the interface in a struct works:
```
groupshared struct { IFoo member; }
```
This suggests the mechanism is feasible. The issue is specifically that `groupshared` directly with an interface type fails.

Let me look at how the struct-wrapping case works differently. In that case, the interface is stored inside a struct, not directly as the variable type. Let me try to understand the IR more.

Actually, let me look at the second error message more carefully. The SPIR-V error says:
```
let  %1 : %2 = lookupWitness(%3, %IFoox5FgetValue)
```

This is a `lookupWitness` on a global symbol. The witness table operand (%3) is the witness table extracted from the existential. The problem is that this lookup can't be resolved at compile time because the witness table is stored in a `groupshared` variable.

Let me check if there's a way to handle this by looking at how normal (non-groupshared) interface variables work. With a regular global variable of interface type, the dispatch should work through the dynamic dispatch mechanism.

Let me look at what happens with a non-groupshared variable to compare

interface IFoo
{
    float getValue();
}

struct ImplA : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v; }
}

struct ImplB : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v * 2.0; }
}

// No groupshared - just a regular global
static IFoo sharedVal;

RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;

[numthreads(64, 1, 1)]
void computeMain(uint3 tid : SV_DispatchThreadID)
{
    if (tid.x == 0)
        sharedVal = ImplA(42.0);
    GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync();
    outputBuffer[tid.x] = sharedVal.getValue();
}

Now I understand the issue better. The diagnostic is about uninitialized existentials. But for globals, the issue is different - the compiler crashes because the lookupWitness remains unresolved.

Let me think about this differently. The real fix should be to either:
1. Properly handle dynamic dispatch for groupshared interface variables
2. Emit a diagnostic that this is not supported

Given the PR description says "Note: wrapping the interface in a struct (groupshared struct { IFoo member; }) compiles and generates correct dynamic dispatch code", the issue is specifically about having an interface type directly in a groupshared variable.

Let me look at how the struct wrapping case works. In that case, the variable is a struct type (not an interface type), and the interface is a field. The existential operations happen on the field.

     1	#include "slang-ir-check-shader-parameter-type.h"
     2	
     3	#include "slang-ir-util.h"
     4	#include "slang-rich-diagnostics.h"
     5	
     6	namespace Slang
     7	{
     8	
     9	template<typename P>
    10	auto isOrContains(P predicate, IRType* type) -> decltype(predicate(type))
    11	{
    12	    HashSet<IRType*> visited;
    13	
    14	    auto go = [&visited, &predicate](auto&& self, IRType* type) -> decltype(predicate(type))
    15	    {
    16	        // Prevent infinite recursion by tracking visited types
    17	        if (!visited.add(type))
    18	            return {};
    19	
    20	        // Check if the current type matches the predicate
    21	        if (auto result = predicate(type))
    22	            return result;
    23	
    24	        // Recursively check struct fields
    25	        if (auto structType = as<IRStructType>(type))
    26	        {
    27	            for (auto field : structType->getFields())
    28	            {
    29	                auto fieldType = field->getFieldType();
    30	                if (auto result = self(self, fieldType))
    31	                    return result;
    32	            }
    33	        }
    34	
    35	        return {};
    36	    };
    37	
    38	    return go(go, type);
    39	}
    40	
    41	void checkForInvalidShaderParameterTypeForMetal(IRModule* module, DiagnosticSink* sink)
    42	{
    43	    auto isConstantBufferWithResource = [](IRType* type) -> std::optional<IRType*>
    44	    {
    45	        if (type->getOp() == kIROp_ConstantBufferType)
    46	        {
    47	            // Get the type inside the constant buffer
    48	            auto innerType = as<IRType>(type->getOperand(0));
    49	
    50	            // Check if the inner type contains any resource types
    51	            auto hasResource = [](IRType* t) -> std::optional<IRType*>
    52	            {
    53	                if (isResourceType(t))
    54	                    return t;
    55	                return {};
    56	            };
    57	
    58	            if (auto resourceType = isOrContains(hasResource, innerType))
    59	                return type; // Return the constant buffer type if it contains a resource
    60	        }
    61	        return {};
    62	    };
    63	
    64	    for (auto inst : module->getGlobalInsts())
    65	    {
    66	        if (inst->getOp() != kIROp_ParameterBlockType)
    67	            continue;
    68	
    69	        auto type = as<IRType>(inst->getOperand(0));
    70	        if (auto invalidCBType = isOrContains(isConstantBufferWithResource, type))
    71	        {
    72	            // Try to find a valid source location from uses
    73	            bool foundUseSite = false;
    74	            for (auto use = inst->firstUse; use; use = use->nextUse)
    75	            {
    76	                auto user = use->getUser();
    77	                if (user->sourceLoc.isValid())
    78	                {
    79	                    sink->diagnose(
    80	                        Diagnostics::ResourceTypesInConstantBufferInParameterBlockNotAllowedOnMetal{
    81	                            .location = user->sourceLoc});
    82	                    foundUseSite = true;
    83	                    break;
    84	                }
    85	            }
    86	
    87	            if (!foundUseSite)
    88	                sink->diagnose(
    89	                    Diagnostics::ResourceTypesInConstantBufferInParameterBlockNotAllowedOnMetal{
    90	                        .location = inst->sourceLoc});
    91	        }
    92	    }
    93	}
    94	void checkForInvalidShaderParameterType(
    95	    IRModule* module,
    96	    TargetRequest* target,
    97	    DiagnosticSink* sink)
    98	{
    99	    if (isMetalTarget(target))
   100	        checkForInvalidShaderParameterTypeForMetal(module, sink);
   101	}
   102	} // namespace Slang
   103	




2026-05-28 18:15:36,337 - INFO - swea-agent - ========================= STEP 68 =========================
2026-05-28 18:15:36,348 - DEBUG - swea-lm - n_cache_control: 2
2026-05-28 18:15:42,372 - DEBUG - swea-lm - Response: ModelResponse(id='3d5a3e47919f4ecbab57367007c7a307', created=1779992138, model='DeepSeek-V4-Pro', object='chat.completion', system_fingerprint=None, choices=[Choices(finish_reason='tool_calls', index=0, message=Message(content="This is not directly related. Let me now focus on the actual fix. Looking at the problem:\n\n1. `groupshared IFoo sharedVal;` creates a global variable with an existentially-typed value\n2. When `sharedVal.getValue()` is called, the compiler needs to:\n   - Extract the witness table from the existential (using `ExtractExistentialWitnessTable`)\n   - Look up the method in the witness table (using `LookupWitnessMethod`)\n3. The `LookupWitnessMethod` can't be resolved because the witness table comes from a dynamic source (the groupshared variable)\n4. The emit passes crash because they can't handle `LookupWitnessMethod`\n\nThe issue is that the typeflow specialization pass (`specializeDynamicInsts`) should either:\n- Lower the `LookupWitnessMethod` to a dynamic dispatch\n- Or leave it alone and let a later pass handle it\n\nBut it seems like neither happens. 2026-05-28 18:15:42,393 - INFO - swea-agent - 💭 THOUGHT
This is not directly related. Let me now focus on the actual fix. Looking at the problem:

1. `groupshared IFoo sharedVal;` creates a global variable with an existentially-typed value
2. When `sharedVal.getValue()` is called, the compiler needs to:
   - Extract the witness table from the existential (using `ExtractExistentialWitnessTable`)
   - Look up the method in the witness table (using `LookupWitnessMethod`)
3. The `LookupWitnessMethod` can't be resolved because the witness table comes from a dynamic source (the groupshared variable)
4. The emit passes crash because they can't handle `LookupWitnessMethod`

The issue is that the typeflow specialization pass (`specializeDynamicInsts`) should either:
- Lower the `LookupWitnessMethod` to a dynamic dispatch
- Or leave it alone and let a later pass handle it

But it seems like neither happens. Let me look at what happens after the specialization pass to see if there's a catch-all for remaining `LookupWitnessMethod` instructions.

Let me check the `slang-ir-lower-com-methods.cpp` which handles COM interface methods:

     5	#include "slang-ir-inst-pass-base.h"

    10	
    11	namespace Slang
    12	{
    13	
    14	struct ComMethodLoweringContext : public InstPassBase
    15	{
    16	    DiagnosticSink* diagnosticSink = nullptr;
    17	
    18	    NativeCallMarshallingContext marshal;
    19	
    20	    OrderedHashSet<IRLookupWitnessMethod*> comCallees;
    21	
    22	    ComMethodLoweringContext(IRModule* inModule)
    23	        : InstPassBase(inModule)
    24	    {
    25	    }
    26	
    27	    void processComCall(IRCall* comCall)
    28	    {
    29	        IRBuilder builder(module);
    30	        builder.setInsertBefore(comCall);
    31	        auto callee = as<IRLookupWitnessMethod>(comCall->getCallee());
    32	        SLANG_ASSERT(callee);
    33	
    34	        IRLookupWitnessMethod* innerMostCallee = callee;
    35	        while (innerMostCallee->getOperand(0)->getOp() == kIROp_LookupWitnessMethod)
    36	        {
    37	            innerMostCallee = as<IRLookupWitnessMethod>(innerMostCallee->getOperand(0));
    38	        }
    39	        if (callee != innerMostCallee)
    40	        {
    41	            callee = (IRLookupWitnessMethod*)builder.emitLookupInterfaceMethodInst(
    42	                callee->getDataType(),
    43	                innerMostCallee->getWitnessTable(),
    44	                callee->getRequirementKey());
    45	        }
    46	        comCallees.add(callee);
    47	
    48	        auto calleeType = as<IRFuncType>(callee->getDataType());
    49	        SLANG_ASSERT(calleeType);
    50	
    51	        auto nativeFuncType = marshal.getNativeFuncType(builder, calleeType);
    52	        ShortList<IRInst*> args;
    53	        for (UInt i = 0; i < comCall->getArgCount(); i++)
    54	            args.add(comCall->getArg(i));
    55	        auto currentBlock = builder.getBlock();
    56	        auto nextInst = comCall->getNextInst();
    57	        auto newResult = marshal.marshalNativeCall(
    58	            builder,
    59	            calleeType,
    60	            nativeFuncType,
    61	            callee,
    62	            args.getCount(),
    63	            args.getArrayView().getBuffer());
    64	
    65	        comCall->replaceUsesWith(newResult);
    66	        if (builder.getBlock() != currentBlock)
    67	        {
    68	            // `marshalNativeCall` may have replaced the original call with branch insts.
    69	            // If this is the case, we need to move all insts after the original call in the
    70	            // original basic block to the new basic block.
    71	            while (nextInst)
    72	            {
    73	                auto next = nextInst->getNextInst();
    74	                nextInst->removeFromParent();
    75	                nextInst->insertAtEnd(builder.getBlock());
    76	                nextInst = next;
    77	            }
    78	        }
    79	        comCall->removeAndDeallocate();
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    void processCall(IRCall* inst)
    83	    {
    84	        auto funcValue = inst->getOperand(0);
    85	
    86	        // Detect if this is a call into a COM interface method.
    87	        if (funcValue->getOp() == kIROp_LookupWitnessMethod)
    88	        {
    89	            const auto operand0TypeOp = funcValue->getOperand(0)->getDataType();
    90	            if (auto tableType = as<IRWitnessTableTypeBase>(operand0TypeOp))

  2060	        // Make a string out of all the dispatch table element's concrete type names.

  2062	        bool first = true;
  2063	        UInt count = 0;
  2064	        forEachInSet(

  2069	                if (first)
  2070	                    first = false;
  2071	                else
  2072	                    tableElementsStr << ", ";
  2073	                auto concreteType = cast<IRWitnessTable>(table)->getConcreteType();
  2074	                printDiagnosticArg(tableElementsStr, concreteType);

  1558	struct ExistentialLoweringContext : public InstPassBase
  1559	{
  1560	    TargetProgram* targetProgram;
  1561	
  1562	    ExistentialLoweringContext(IRModule* module, TargetProgram* targetProgram)
  1563	        : InstPassBase(module), targetProgram(targetProgram)
  1564	    {
  1565	    }
  1566	
  1567	    bool _canReplace(IRUse* use)
  1568	    {
  1569	        switch (use->getUser()->getOp())
  1570	        {
  1571	        case kIROp_WitnessTableIDType:
  1572	        case kIROp_WitnessTableType:
  1573	        case kIROp_RTTIPointerType:
  1574	        case kIROp_RTTIHandleType:
  1575	        case kIROp_ComPtrType:
  1576	        case kIROp_NativePtrType:
  1577	            {
  1578	                // Don't replace
  1579	                return false;
  1580	            }
  1581	        case kIROp_ThisType:
  1582	            {
  1583	                // Appears replacable.
  1584	                break;
  1585	            }
  1586	        case kIROp_PtrType:
  1587	            {
  1588	                // We can have ** and ComPtr<T>*.
  1589	                // If it's a pointer type it could be because it is a global.
  1590	                break;
  1591	            }
  1592	        default:
  1593	            break;
  1594	        }
  1595	        return true;
  1596	    }
  1597	
  1598	    // Replace all WitnessTableID type or RTTIHandleType with `uint2`.
  1599	    void lowerHandleTypes()
  1600	    {
  1601	        List<IRInst*> instsToRemove;
  1602	        for (auto inst : module->getGlobalInsts())
  1603	        {
  1604	            switch (inst->getOp())
  1605	            {
  1606	            case kIROp_WitnessTableIDType:
  1607	                if (isComInterfaceType((IRType*)inst->getOperand(0)))
  1608	                    continue;
  1609	                // fall through
  1610	            case kIROp_RTTIHandleType:
  1611	                {
  1612	                    IRBuilder builder(module);
  1613	                    builder.setInsertBefore(inst);
  1614	                    auto uint2Type = builder.getVectorType(
  1615	                        builder.getUIntType(),
  1616	                        builder.getIntValue(builder.getIntType(), 2));
  1617	                    inst->replaceUsesWith(uint2Type);
  1618	                    instsToRemove.add(inst);
  1619	                }
  1620	                break;
  1621	            }
  1622	        }
  1623	        for (auto inst : instsToRemove)
  1624	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  1625	    }
  1626	
  1627	    IRInst* lowerInterfaceType(IRInst* interfaceType)
  1628	    {
  1629	        if (isComInterfaceType((IRType*)interfaceType))
  1630	            return (IRType*)interfaceType;
  1631	
  1632	        IRBuilder builder(module);
  1633	        if (isBuiltin(interfaceType))
  1634	            return (IRType*)builder.getIntValue(builder.getIntType(), 0);
  1635	
  1636	        // In the dynamic-dispatch case, a value of interface type
  1637	        // is going to be packed into the "any value" part of a tuple.
  1638	        // The size of the "any value" part depends on the interface
  1639	        // type (e.g., it might have an `[anyValueSize(8)]` attribute
  1640	        // indicating that 8 bytes needs to be reserved).
  1641	        //
  1642	
  1643	        IRIntegerValue anyValueSize = 0;
  1644	        if (auto decor = interfaceType->findDecoration<IRAnyValueSizeDecoration>())
  1645	        {
  1646	            anyValueSize = decor->getSize();
  1647	        }
  1648	
  1649	        auto anyValueType = builder.getAnyValueType(anyValueSize);
  1650	        auto witnessTableType = builder.getWitnessTableIDType((IRType*)interfaceType);
  1651	        auto rttiType = builder.getRTTIHandleType();
  1652	
  1653	        // In the ordinary (dynamic) case, an existential type decomposes
  1654	        // into a tuple of:
  1655	        //
  1656	        //      (RTTI, witness table, any-value).
  1657	        //
  1658	        return builder.getTupleType(rttiType, witnessTableType, anyValueType);
  1659	    }
  1660
