  1660	
  1661	    IRInst* lowerBoundInterfaceType(IRBoundInterfaceType* boundInterfaceType)
  1662	    {
  1663	        // A bound interface type represents an existential together with
  1664	        // static knowledge that the value stored in the extistential has
  1665	        // a particular concrete type.
  1666	        //
  1667	
  1668	        IRBuilder builder(module);
  1669	
  1670	        auto payloadType = boundInterfaceType->getConcreteType();
  1671	        auto witnessTableType = builder.getWitnessTableIDType(
  1672	            (IRType*)as<IRWitnessTable>(boundInterfaceType->getWitnessTable())
  1673	                ->getConformanceType());
  1674	        auto rttiType = builder.getRTTIHandleType();
  1675	        auto interfaceType = boundInterfaceType->getInterfaceType();
  1676	
  1677	        IRIntegerValue anyValueSize = 16;
  1678	        if (auto decor = interfaceType->findDecoration<IRAnyValueSizeDecoration>())
  1679	        {
  1680	            anyValueSize = decor->getSize();
  1681	        }
  1682	
  1683	        auto anyValueType = builder.getAnyValueType(anyValueSize);
  1684	
  1685	        // Because static specialization is being used (at least in part),
  1686	        // we do *not* have a guarantee that the `concreteType` is one
  1687	        // that can fit into the `anyValueSize` of the interface.
  1688	        //
  1689	        // We will use the IR layout logic to see if we can compute
  1690	        // a size for the type, which can lead to a few different outcomes:
  1691	        //
  1692	        // * If a size is computed successfully, and it is smaller than or
  1693	        //   equal to `anyValueSize`, then the concrete value will fit into
  1694	        //   the reserved area, and the layout will match the dynamic case.
  1695	        //
  1696	        // * If a size is computed successfully, and it is larger than
  1697	        //   `anyValueSize`, then the concrete value cannot fit into the
  1698	        //   reserved area, and it needs to be stored out-of-line.
  1699	        //
  1700	        // * If size cannot be computed, then that implies that the type
  1701	        //   includes non-ordinary data (e.g., a `Texture2D` on a D3D11
  1702	        //   target), and cannot possible fit into the reserved area
  1703	        //   (which consists of only uniform bytes). In this case, the
  1704	        //   value must be stored out-of-line.
  1705	        //
  1706	        IRSizeAndAlignment sizeAndAlignment;
  1707	        Result result = getNaturalSizeAndAlignment(
  1708	            targetProgram->getTargetReq(),
  1709	            payloadType,
  1710	            &sizeAndAlignment);
  1711	        if (SLANG_FAILED(result) || sizeAndAlignment.size > anyValueSize)
  1712	        {
  1713	            // In the case where static specialization mandateds out-of-line storage,
  1714	            // an existential type decomposes into a tuple of:
  1715	            //
  1716	            //      (RTTI, witness table, pseudo pointer, any-value)
  1717	            //
  1718	            return builder.getTupleType(
  1719	                rttiType,
  1720	                witnessTableType,
  1721	                builder.getPseudoPtrType(payloadType),
  1722	                anyValueType);
  1723	        }
  1724	        else
  1725	        {
  1726	            // Regular case (lower in the same way as unbound interface types)
  1727	            return builder.getTupleType(rttiType, witnessTableType, anyValueType);
  1728	        }
  1729	    }
  1730	
  1731	    bool lowerExtractExistentialType(IRExtractExistentialType* inst)
  1732	    {
  1733	        // Replace with extraction of the type as a value from the existential tuple.
  1734	        //
  1735	
  1736	        IRBuilder builder(module);
  1737	        builder.setInsertAfter(inst);
  1738	
  1739	        if (auto tupleType = as<IRTupleType>(inst->getOperand(0)->getDataType()))
  1740	        {
  1741	            inst->replaceUsesWith(builder.emitGetTupleElement(
  1742	                (IRType*)tupleType->getOperand(0),
  1743	                inst->getOperand(0),
  1744	                0));
  1745	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  1746	        }
  1747	        else if (isEffectivelyComPtrType((IRType*)inst->getOperand(0)->getDataType()))
  1748	        {
  1749	            inst->replaceUsesWith(inst->getOperand(0));
  1750	            inst->removeAndDeallocate();

  1935	    void processModule()
  1936	    {
  1937	        // Then, start lowering the remaining non-COM/non-Builtin interface types
  1938	        // At this point, we should only be dealing with public facing uses of
  1939	        // interface types, as all internal uses would have been rewritten
  1940	        // into known tagged-union types.
  1941	        //
  1942	        // These must lower into either a
  1943	        // TupleType(RTTI, witness table ID, AnyValue) for regular interface types or a
  1944	        // TupleType(RTTI, witness table ID, PseudoPtr, AnyValue) for bound interface types.
  1945	        //
  1946	
  1947	        processInstsOfType<IRInterfaceType>(
  1948	            kIROp_InterfaceType,
  1949	            [&](IRInterfaceType* inst)
  1950	            {
  1951	                IRBuilder builder(module);
  1952	                builder.setInsertInto(module);
  1953	                if (auto loweredInterfaceType = lowerInterfaceType(inst))
  1954	                {
  1955	                    if (loweredInterfaceType != inst)
  1956	                    {
  1957	
  1958	                        traverseUses(
  1959	                            inst,
  1960	                            [&](IRUse* use)
  1961	                            {
  1962	                                if (_canReplace(use))
  1963	                                    builder.replaceOperand(use, loweredInterfaceType);
  1964	                            });
  1965	                    }
  1966	                }
  1967	            });
  1968	
  1969	        processInstsOfType<IRBoundInterfaceType>(
  1970	            kIROp_BoundInterfaceType,
  1971	            [&](IRBoundInterfaceType* inst)
  1972	            {
  1973	                IRBuilder builder(module);
  1974	                builder.setInsertInto(module);
  1975	                if (auto loweredBoundInterfaceType = lowerBoundInterfaceType(inst))
  1976	                {
  1977	                    if (loweredBoundInterfaceType != inst)
  1978	                    {
  1979	                        traverseUses(
  1980	                            inst,
  1981	                            [&](IRUse* use)
  1982	                            {
  1983	                                if (_canReplace(use))
  1984	                                    builder.replaceOperand(use, loweredBoundInterfaceType);
  1985	                            });
  1986	                    }
  1987	                }
  1988	            });
  1989	
  1990	        // Replace any other uses with dummy value 0.
  1991	        // TODO: Ideally, we should replace it with OpPoison?
  1992	        {
  1993	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  1994	            builder.setInsertInto(module);
  1995	            auto dummyInterfaceObj = builder.getIntValue(builder.getIntType(), 0);
  1996	            processInstsOfType<IRInterfaceType>(
  1997	                kIROp_InterfaceType,
  1998	                [&](IRInterfaceType* inst)
  1999	                {
  2000	                    if (!isComInterfaceType((IRType*)inst))
  2001	                    {
  2002	                        inst->replaceUsesWith(dummyInterfaceObj);
  2003	                        inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  2004	                    }
  2005	                });
  2006	        }
  2007	
  2008	        processAllInsts(
  2009	            [&](IRInst* inst)
  2010	            {
  2011	                switch (inst->getOp())
  2012	                {
  2013	                case kIROp_ExtractExistentialType:
  2014	                    lowerExtractExistentialType(cast<IRExtractExistentialType>(inst));
  2015	                    break;
  2016	                case kIROp_ExtractExistentialValue:
  2017	                    lowerExtractExistentialValue(cast<IRExtractExistentialValue>(inst));
  2018	                    break;
  2019	                case kIROp_ExtractExistentialWitnessTable:
  2020	                    lowerExtractExistentialWitnessTable(
  2021	                        cast<IRExtractExistentialWitnessTable>(inst));
  2022	                    break;
  2023	                case kIROp_CreateExistentialObject:
  2024	                    // Should have been removed during tagged-union lowering.
  2025	                    lowerCreateExistentialObject(cast<IRCreateExistentialObject>(inst));
  2026	                    break;
  2027	                case kIROp_GetValueFromBoundInterface:
  2028	                    lowerGetValueFromBoundInterface(cast<IRGetValueFromBoundInterface>(inst));
  2029	                    break;
  2030	                }
  2031	            });
  2032	
  2033	        lowerIsTypeInsts(module);
  2034	
  2035	        processInstsOfType<IRGetSequentialID>(
  2036	            kIROp_GetSequentialID,
  2037	            [&](IRGetSequentialID* inst) { return processGetSequentialIDInst(inst); });
  2038	
  2039	        lowerHandleTypes();
  2040	    }
  2041	};
  2042	
  2043	bool lowerExistentials(IRModule* module, TargetProgram* targetProgram, DiagnosticSink* sink)
  2044	{
  2045	    SLANG_UNUSED(sink);
  2046	    ExistentialLoweringContext context(module, targetProgram);
  2047	    context.processModule();
  2048	    return true;
  2049	};

Now I understand the full picture. The flow is:

1. `specializeDynamicInsts` (typeflow specialization) tries to resolve dynamic dispatch
2. `lowerExistentials` replaces interface types with tuple types and handles extract/witness operations

But the issue is that `LookupWitnessMethod` remains unresolved after these passes. Let me check if `lowerExistentials` handles `LookupWitnessMethod`:

Looking at the code above, it handles `ExtractExistentialValue`, `ExtractExistentialType`, `ExtractExistentialWitnessTable`, `CreateExistentialObject`, and `GetValueFromBoundInterface`. But it does NOT handle `LookupWitnessMethod` directly.

So the problem is that `LookupWitnessMethod` should have been resolved by `specializeDynamicInsts` but wasn't. Let me look at what happens when `specializeDynamicInsts` can't resolve it. 

Let me check the `specializeDynamicInsts` flow more carefully to understand why it fails for this case:

  7713	    bool processModule()
  7714	    {
  7715	        bool hasChanges = false;
  7716	
  7717	        // Part 1: Information Propagation
  7718	        //    This phase propagates type information through the module
  7719	        //    and records them into different maps in the current context.
  7720	        //
  7721	        performInformationPropagation();
  7722	
  7723	        if (sink->getErrorCount() > 0)
  7724	        {
  7725	            // If there are any diagnostics after propagation, don't continue into
  7726	            // the mutating lowering phase.
  7727	            return false;
  7728	        }
  7729	
  7730	        // Part 2: Dynamic Instruction Specialization
  7731	        //    Re-write dynamic instructions into specialized versions based on the
  7732	        //    type information in the previous phase.
  7733	        //

  7735	
  7736	        return hasChanges;
  7737	    }

  5115	    bool performDynamicInstLowering()
  5116	    {
  5117	        WorkQueue<IRInst*> globalWorkList;
  5118	        List<IRStructType*> structsToProcess;
  5119	
  5120	        for (auto inst : module->getGlobalInsts())
  5121	        {
  5122	            if (auto func = as<IRFunc>(inst))
  5123	                if (isEntryPoint(func))
  5124	                    globalWorkList.enqueue(func);
  5125	
  5126	            if (auto structType = as<IRStructType>(inst))
  5127	                structsToProcess.add(structType);
  5128	        }
  5129	
  5130	        bool hasChanges = false;
  5131	
  5132	        // Lower struct types first so that data access can be
  5133	        // marshalled properly during func specializeing.
  5134	        //
  5135	        for (auto structType : structsToProcess)
  5136	            hasChanges |= specializeStructType(structType);
  5137	
  5138	        HashSet<IRInst*> processedSet;
  5139	        while (globalWorkList.hasItems())
  5140	        {
  5141	            if (sink->getErrorCount() > 0)
  5142	                break;
  5143	
  5144	            auto globalInst = globalWorkList.dequeue();
  5145	
  5146	            switch (globalInst->getOp())
  5147	            {
  5148	            case kIROp_Func:
  5149	                {
  5150	                    auto func = as<IRFunc>(globalInst);
  5151	                    if (processedSet.contains(globalInst))
  5152	                        continue;
  5153	
  5154	                    hasChanges |= removeAnnotations(func);
  5155	                    hasChanges |= eliminateDeadCode(func);
  5156	                    hasChanges |= specializeFunc(func, globalWorkList);
  5157	
  5158	                    if (sink->getErrorCount() > 0)
  5159	                        break;
  5160	
  5161	                    hasChanges |= eliminateDeadCode(func);
  5162	                    hasChanges |= resolveTypesInFunc(func);
  5163	
  5164	                    processedSet.add(globalInst);
  5165	                    break;
  5166	                }
  5167	            case kIROp_Specialize:
  5168	                {
  5169	                    // Resolve and replace.
  5170	                    translationContext.resolveInst(globalInst);
  5171	                    break;
  5172	                }
  5173	            default:
  5174	                SLANG_UNEXPECTED("Unexpected global inst type during specialization");
  5175	            }
  5176	        }
  5177	
  5178	        return hasChanges;
  5179	    }
  5180	
  5181	    // Returns an effective type to use for an inst, given its
  5182	    // info.
  5183	    //
  5184	    // This basically recursively walks the info and applies the array/ptr-type
  5185	    // wrappers, while replacing unbounded set with a nullptr.
  5186	    //
  5187	    // If the result of this is null, then the inst should keep its current type.
  5188	    //
  5189	    IRType* getLoweredType(IRInst* info)
  5190	    {
  5191	        if (!info)
  5192	            return nullptr;
  5193	
  5194	        if (auto attributedType = as<IRAttributedType>(info))
  5195	        {
  5196	            if (auto specializedValueType = getLoweredType(attributedType->getBaseType()))
  5197	            {
  5198	                IRBuilder builder(module);
  5199	
  5200	                List<IRAttr*>& attrs = *module->getContainerPool().getList<IRAttr>();
  5201	                for (auto attr : attributedType->getAllAttrs())
  5202	                    attrs.add(attr);
  5203	
  5204	                auto newAttributedType =
  5205	                    builder.getAttributedType((IRType*)specializedValueType, attrs);
  5206	                module->getContainerPool().free(&attrs);
  5207	                return newAttributedType;
  5208	            }
  5209	            else
  5210	                return nullptr;
  5211	        }
  5212	
  5213	        if (auto ptrType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(info))
  5214	        {
  5215	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  5216	            if (auto specializedValueType = getLoweredType(ptrType->getValueType()))
  5217	            {
  5218	                return builder.getPtrTypeWithAddressSpace((IRType*)specializedValueType, ptrType);
  5219	            }
  5220	            else
  5221	                return nullptr;
  5222	        }
  5223	
  5224	        if (auto arrayType = as<IRArrayType>(info))
  5225	        {
  5226	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  5227	            if (auto specializedElementType = getLoweredType(arrayType->getElementType()))
  5228	            {
  5229	                return builder.getArrayType(
  5230	                    (IRType*)specializedElementType,
  5231	                    arrayType->getElementCount(),
  5232	                    getArrayStride(arrayType));
  5233	            }
  5234	            else
  5235	                return nullptr;
  5236	        }
  5237	
  5238	        if (auto tupleType = as<IRTupleType>(info))
  5239	        {
  5240	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  5241	            List<IRType*> specializedElements;
  5242	            bool anySpecialized = false;
  5243	            for (UInt i = 0; i < tupleType->getOperandCount(); i++)
  5244	            {
  5245	                if (auto specializedElementType = getLoweredType((IRType*)tupleType->getOperand(i)))
  5246	                {
  5247	                    specializedElements.add(specializedElementType);
  5248	                    anySpecialized = true;
  5249	                }
  5250	                else
  5251	                {
  5252	                    specializedElements.add((IRType*)tupleType->getOperand(i));
  5253	                }
  5254	            }
  5255	            if (anySpecialized || tupleType->getOperandCount() == 0)
  5256	                return builder.getTupleType(specializedElements);
  5257	            else
  5258	                return nullptr;
  5259	        }
  5260	
  5261	        if (auto optionalType = as<IROptionalType>(info))
  5262	        {
  5263	            IRBuilder builder(module);
  5264	            if (auto specializedValueType = getLoweredType(optionalType->getValueType()))
  5265	            {
  5266	                return builder.getOptionalType((IRType*)specializedValueType);
  5267	            }
  5268	            else
  5269	                return nullptr;
  5270	        }
  5271	
  5272	        if (auto taggedUnion = as<IRTaggedUnionType>(info))
  5273	        {
  5274	            return (IRType*)taggedUnion;
  5275	        }
  5276	
  5277	        if (auto elementOfSetType = as<IRElementOfSetType>(info))
  5278	        {
  5279	            // Replace element-of-set types with tag types.
  5280	            return makeTagType(elementOfSetType->getSet());
  5281	        }
  5282	
  5283	        if (auto valOfSetType = as<IRUntaggedUnionType>(info))
  5284	        {
  5285	            if (valOfSetType->getSet()->isSingleton())
  5286	            {
  5287	                // If there's only one type in the set, return it directly
  5288	                return (IRType*)valOfSetType->getSet()->getElement(0);
  5289	            }
  5290	
  5291	            return valOfSetType;
  5292	        }
  5293	
  5294	        if (as<IRFuncSet>(info) || as<IRWitnessTableSet>(info))
  5295	        {
  5296	            // Don't specialize these sets.. they should be used through
  5297	            // tag types, or be processed out during specializeing.
  5298	            //
  5299	            return nullptr;
  5300	        }
  5301	
  5302	        return (IRType*)info;
  5303	    }
  5304	
  5305	    // Replace an insts type with its effective type as determined by the analysis.
  5306	    bool replaceType(IRInst* context, IRInst* inst)
  5307	    {
  5308	        // If the inst is a global val, we won't modify it.
  5309	        if (as<IRModuleInst>(inst->getParent()))
  5310	        {
  5311	            if (as<IRType>(inst) || as<IRWitnessTable>(inst) || as<IRFunc>(inst) ||
  5312	                as<IRGeneric>(inst))
  5313	            {
  5314	                return false;
  5315	            }
  5316	        }
  5317	
  5318	        if (auto info = tryGetInfo(context, inst))
  5319	        {
  5320	            if (auto specializedType = getLoweredType(info))
  5321	            {
  5322	                if (specializedType == inst->getDataType())
  5323	                    return false; // No change
  5324	                inst->setFullType(specializedType);
  5325	                return true;
  5326	            }
  5327	        }
  5328	        return false;
  5329	    }
  5330	
  5331	    bool specializeInst(IRInst* context, IRInst* inst, WorkQueue<IRInst*>& globalsWorkList)
  5332	    {
  5333	        switch (inst->getOp())
  5334	        {
  5335	        case kIROp_LookupWitnessMethod:
  5336	            return specializeLookupWitnessMethod(context, as<IRLookupWitnessMethod>(inst));
  5337	        case kIROp_ExtractExistentialWitnessTable:
  5338	            return specializeExtractExistentialWitnessTable(
  5339	                context,
  5340	                as<IRExtractExistentialWitnessTable>(inst));
  5341	        case kIROp_ExtractExistentialType:
  5342	            return specializeExtractExistentialType(context, as<IRExtractExistentialType>(inst));
  5343	        case kIROp_ExtractExistentialValue:
  5344	            return specializeExtractExistentialValue(context, as<IRExtractExistentialValue>(inst));
  5345	        case kIROp_Call:

  5488	    bool specializeExtractExistentialWitnessTable(
  5489	        IRInst* context,
  5490	        IRExtractExistentialWitnessTable* inst)
  5491	    {
  5492	        // If we have a non-trivial info registered, it must of
  5493	        // SetTagType(WitnessTableSet(...))
  5494	        //
  5495	        // Further, the operand must be an existential (TaggedUnionType), which is
  5496	        // conceptually a pair of TagType(tableSet) and a
  5497	        // UntaggedUnionType(typeSet)
  5498	        //
  5499	        // We will simply extract the first element of this tuple.
  5500	        //
  5501	
  5502	        auto info = tryGetInfo(context, inst);
  5503	        if (!info)
  5504	            return false;
  5505	
  5506	        IRBuilder builder(inst);
  5507	        builder.setInsertBefore(inst);
  5508	
  5509	        if (auto elementOfSetType = as<IRElementOfSetType>(info))
  5510	        {
  5511	            if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isSingleton())
  5512	            {
  5513	                // Found a single possible type. Simple replacement.
  5514	                inst->replaceUsesWith(elementOfSetType->getSet()->getElement(0));
  5515	                inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5516	                return true;
  5517	            }
  5518	            else if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isUnbounded())
  5519	            {
  5520	                // We'll leave extracts from unbounded sets alone.
  5521	                // This typically implies COM interfaces or other run-time dynamic
  5522	                // lookups. These are further handled in lowerComInterfaces()
  5523	                //
  5524	                return false;
  5525	            }
  5526	            else if (elementOfSetType->getSet()->isEmpty())
  5527	            {
  5528	                inst->replaceUsesWith(builder.emitPoison(inst->getDataType()));
  5529	                inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5530	                return true;
  5531	            }
  5532	            else
  5533	            {
  5534	                // Replace with GetElement(specializedInst, 0) -> TagType(tableSet)
  5535	                // which retreives a 'tag' (i.e. a run-time identifier for one of the elements
  5536	                // of the set)
  5537	                //
  5538	                auto operand = inst->getOperand(0);
  5539	                auto element = builder.emitGetTagFromTaggedUnion(operand);
  5540	                inst->replaceUsesWith(element);
  5541	                inst->removeAndDeallocate();
  5542	                return true;
  5543	            }
  5544	        }
  5545	        else
  5546	        {
  5547	            SLANG_UNEXPECTED("Unexpected info type for ExtractExistentialWitnessTable");
  5548	        }
  5549	    }
  5550

  2306	    IRInst* analyzeLoad(IRInst* context, IRInst* inst)
  2307	    {
  2308	        IRBuilder builder(module);
  2309	        if (auto loadInst = as<IRLoad>(inst))
  2310	        {
  2311	            // If we have a simple load, theres one of two cases:
  2312	            //
  2313	            // 1. If we're loading from a resource pointer, we need to treat it
  2314	            //    as unspecializable. If it's a COM interface, we consider it truly
  2315	            //    unbounded. Otherwise, we can simply enumerate all tables for the interface
  2316	            //    type.
  2317	            //
  2318	            // 2. In the default case, we can look up the registered information
  2319	            //    for the pointer, which should be of the form PtrTypeBase(valueInfo), and
  2320	            //    use the valueInfo
  2321	            //
  2322	            auto findGlobalTables = [&](IRInterfaceType* interfaceType) -> IRInst*
  2323	            {
  2324	                HashSet<IRInst*>& tables = *module->getContainerPool().getHashSet<IRInst>();
  2325	                collectExistentialTables(interfaceType, tables);
  2326	                if (tables.getCount() > 0)
  2327	                {
  2328	                    auto resultTaggedUnionType = makeTaggedUnionType(
  2329	                        as<IRWitnessTableSet>(builder.getSet(kIROp_WitnessTableSet, tables)));
  2330	                    module->getContainerPool().free(&tables);
  2331	                    return resultTaggedUnionType;
  2332	                }
  2333	                else
  2334	                {
  2335	                    StringBuilder typeStr;
  2336	                    printDiagnosticArg(typeStr, interfaceType);
  2337	                    sink->diagnose(Diagnostics::NoTypeConformancesFoundForInterface{
  2338	                        .interfaceType = typeStr.produceString(),
  2339	                        .location = loadInst->sourceLoc});
  2340	                    module->getContainerPool().free(&tables);
  2341	                    return none();
  2342	                }
  2343	            };
  2344	
  2345	            if (isResourcePointer(loadInst->getPtr()))
  2346	            {
  2347	                if (auto interfaceType = as<IRInterfaceType>(loadInst->getDataType()))
  2348	                {
  2349	                    if (!isComInterfaceType(interfaceType))
  2350	                    {
  2351	                        return findGlobalTables(interfaceType);
  2352	                    }
  2353	                    else
  2354	                    {
  2355	                        return none();
  2356	                    }
  2357	                }
  2358	                else if (
  2359	                    auto boundInterfaceType = as<IRBoundInterfaceType>(loadInst->getDataType()))
  2360	                {
  2361	                    return makeTaggedUnionType(cast<IRWitnessTableSet>(builder.getSingletonSet(
  2362	                        kIROp_WitnessTableSet,
  2363	                        boundInterfaceType->getWitnessTable())));
  2364	                }
  2365	                else
  2366	                {
  2367	                    // Loading from a resource pointer that isn't a direct interface?s
  2368	                    // Just return no info.
  2369	                    return none();
  2370	                }
  2371	            }
  2372	
  2373	            // If the load is from a pointer, we can transfer the info directly
  2374	            auto address = as<IRLoad>(loadInst)->getPtr();
  2375	            if (auto addrInfo = tryGetInfo(context, address))
  2376	            {
  2377	                auto valueInfo = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(addrInfo)->getValueType();
  2378	                return valueInfo;
  2379	            }
  2380	            else
  2381	                return none(); // No info for the address
  2382	        }
  2383	        else if (as<IRRWStructuredBufferLoad>(inst) || as<IRStructuredBufferLoad>(inst))
  2384	        {
  2385	            // In case of a buffer load, we know we're dealing with a location that cannot
  2386	            // be specialized, so the logic is similar to case (1) from above.
  2387	            //
  2388	            if (auto interfaceType = as<IRInterfaceType>(inst->getDataType()))
  2389	            {
  2390	                if (!isComInterfaceType(interfaceType))
  2391	                {
  2392	                    HashSet<IRInst*>& tables = *module->getContainerPool().getHashSet<IRInst>();
  2393	                    collectExistentialTables(interfaceType, tables);
  2394	                    if (tables.getCount() > 0)
  2395	                    {
  2396	                        auto resultTaggedUnionType = makeTaggedUnionType(
  2397	                            as<IRWitnessTableSet>(builder.getSet(kIROp_WitnessTableSet, tables)));
  2398	                        module->getContainerPool().free(&tables);
  2399	                        return resultTaggedUnionType;
  2400	                    }
  2401	                    else
  2402	                    {
  2403	                        module->getContainerPool().free(&tables);
  2404	                        return none();
  2405	                    }
  2406	                }
  2407	                else
  2408	                {
  2409	                    return none();
  2410	                }
  2411	            }
  2412	            else if (auto boundInterfaceType = as<IRBoundInterfaceType>(inst->getDataType()))
  2413	            {
  2414	                return makeTaggedUnionType(cast<IRWitnessTableSet>(builder.getSingletonSet(
  2415	                    kIROp_WitnessTableSet,
  2416	                    boundInterfaceType->getWitnessTable())));
  2417	            }
  2418	        }
  2419	
  2420	        return none(); // No info for other load types
  2421	    }
  2422

  2430	        //

The key line is 163-164: `if (as<IRGlobalParam>(inst)) return true;` - global parameters are considered resource pointers. Similarly, global variables might be treated the same way.

Let me look at how `isResourcePointer` is used in `analyzeLoad` (line 2345). If the pointer is a resource pointer, it goes into a special path that only handles interface types (line 2347-2356) or bound interface types. For a regular `IRGlobalParam` with interface type, it would:

1. Check `isResourcePointer` → true (because it's a global param)
2. Check for `IRInterfaceType` → true
3. Check `isComInterfaceType` → false (for regular interfaces)
4. Call `findGlobalTables(interfaceType)` which collects all conformances

This should work! Let me check if `groupshared` variables are represented as `IRGlobalParam` as well:

   275	bool isPointerToResourceType(IRType* type)
   276	{
   277	    while (auto ptrType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(type))
   278	    {
   279	        if (ptrType->getAddressSpace() == AddressSpace::StorageBuffer ||
   280	            ptrType->getAddressSpace() == AddressSpace::UserPointer)
   281	            return true;
   282	        type = ptrType->getValueType();
   283	    }
   284	
   285	    return isResourceType(type);
   286	}
   287	
   288	ModuleDecl* findModuleForDecl(Decl* decl)
   289	{
   290	    for (auto dd = decl; dd; dd = dd->parentDecl)
   291	    {
   292	        if (auto moduleDecl = as<ModuleDecl>(dd))
   293	            return moduleDecl;
   294	    }
   295	    return nullptr;
   296	}

   299	// Helper type for legalization of aggregate types

   305	    IRStructType* originalStructType;

Now let me also check if there's something else we should add. The `RateQualified(GroupShared, Ptr(%IFoo))` type might be another path. Let me check if there's a similar handling for `IRGlobalVar`:

Actually, wait. Let me look at the actual issue differently. In `analyzeLoad`:

```cpp
if (isResourcePointer(loadInst->getPtr()))
{
    if (auto interfaceType = as<IRInterfaceType>(loadInst->getDataType()))
    {
        if (!isComInterfaceType(interfaceType))
        {
            return findGlobalTables(interfaceType);
        }
        else
        {
            return none();
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

So if the pointer is a resource pointer AND the loaded type is an interface type, it finds all conforming witness tables and creates a tagged union type from them. This is exactly what we need.

If the pointer is NOT recognized as a resource pointer, it falls through to:
```cpp
auto address = as<IRLoad>(loadInst)->getPtr();
if (auto addrInfo = tryGetInfo(context, address))
{
    auto valueInfo = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(addrInfo)->getValueType();
    return valueInfo;
}
else
    return none(); // No info for the address
```

So if we can't get info for the address, we return `none()`. This is likely what happens for `groupshared` variables - the address doesn't have any info (since `IRGlobalVar` doesn't participate in the typeflow analysis), so we get `none()`.

The fix should be to treat `IRGlobalVar` (and possibly `IRGlobalParam` with `GroupShared` address space) as resource pointers. Let me check if this fixes the issue.

But wait, I also need to check if `IRGlobalVar` variables get info propagated to them. Let me look at how the analysis handles `IRGlobalVar`:

Actually, let me re-examine. The `analyzeLoad` function is called for `IRLoad` instructions. The `loadInst->getPtr()` for a load from `sharedVal` would be the `IRGlobalVar` itself (or a pointer to it). Let me check...

Actually, looking at the IR dump above: `let %170 : %IFoo = load(%sharedVal)`. So the pointer operand of the load is `%sharedVal` which is a `global_var`. So `loadInst->getPtr()` returns the `IRGlobalVar`.

Now `isResourcePointer(IRGlobalVar*)` checks:
1. `isPointerToResourceType(IRGlobalVar->getDataType())` - the type is `RateQualified(GroupShared, Ptr(%IFoo))`. Let me check if this is considered a pointer to resource type...

Actually, looking at `isPointerToResourceType`:
```cpp
bool isPointerToResourceType(IRType* type)
{
    while (auto ptrType = as<IRPtrTypeBase>(type))
    {
        if (ptrType->getAddressSpace() == AddressSpace::StorageBuffer ||
            ptrType->getAddressSpace() == AddressSpace::UserPointer)
            return true;
        type = ptrType->getValueType();
    }
    return isResourceType(type);
}
```

This checks for `StorageBuffer` and `UserPointer` but NOT `GroupShared`. And it also unwraps through `IRPtrTypeBase` to check the value type. But the `IRGlobalVar`'s type is wrapped in `RateQualified`, so the `IRPtrTypeBase` check might not even match.

So the fix could be:
1. Add `IRGlobalVar` to `isResourcePointer` (similar to `IRGlobalParam`)
2. Or add `GroupShared` address space to `isPointerToResourceType`

Let me try option 1 first as it seems more targeted

cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/test_groupshared_array.slang << 'EOF'
interface IFoo
{
    float getValue();
}

struct ImplA : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v; }
}

struct ImplB : IFoo
{
    float v;
    float getValue() { return v * 2.0; }
}

groupshared IFoo sharedVal[4];

RWStructuredBuffer<float> outputBuffer;

[numthreads(64, 1, 1)]
void computeMain(uint3 tid : SV_DispatchThreadID)
{
    if (tid.x == 0)
        sharedVal[0] = ImplA(42.0);
    GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync();
    outputBuffer[tid.x] = sharedVal[0].getValue();
}
EOF
